6	// vi.mock is hoisted — factory must NOT reference module-scope variables

22	beforeEach(() => {
23	  useCreateBlockNote.mockClear()

63	| `kanban/client/src/components/Board/Column.test.jsx` | Added 2 tests: `renders the footer prop` and `renders nothing extra when footer prop is not provided` |

68

70	
71	| # | Command | Source |
72	|---|---------|--------|
73	| 1 | `npm -w client run test` | `client/package.json` → `scripts.test` |
74	| 2 | `npm -w client run lint` | `client/package.json` → `scripts.lint` |

83	## 4. Command Results
84	
85	### 4.1 `npm -w client run test` — PASS (with pre-existing warnings)
86	
87	**Exit code:** 0
88	
89	**Summary:**
90	```
91	Test Files  10 passed (10)
92	Tests       328 passed (328)
93	Duration    ~5.9s
94	```
95	
96	All 328 tests pass across 10 test files. The new `CreateCardForm.test.jsx` contributes 33 tests, all green.
97	
98	**Warnings (stderr — pre-existing, not introduced by task 12):**
99	Four tests in `src/hooks/useBoard.test.js` emit React `act(...)` warnings during the "WebSocket integration — initialization" and "multi-client state consistency" suites:
100

102	Warning: An update to TestComponent inside a test was not wrapped in act(...).
103	  > WebSocket integration — initialization > calls useWebSocket with a ws:// URL containing /ws
104	  > WebSocket integration — initialization > subscribes to all 5 event types
105	  > WebSocket integration — initialization > passes an onEvent callback to useWebSocket
106	  > multi-client state consistency > refetches board state when WebSocket reconnects after disconnect
107	```
108	
109	These are logged to stderr but do not fail the test suite. They originate from `useBoard.test.js` (unchanged by task 12) and are a carry-over from a prior task.

128	dist/index.html                   0.48 kB │ gzip:  0.30 kB
129	dist/assets/index-Ndzc9fes.css    5.87 kB │ gzip:  1.63 kB
130	dist/assets/index-DygLZYqf.js   158.22 kB │ gzip: 50.49 kB
131	✓ built in 255ms
132	```

139	
140	**Exit code:** 1
141	
142	**Summary:**
143	```
144	# tests 25 (only ws.test.mjs ran)
145	# pass 21
146	# fail 4
147	```
148	
149	Four server test files (`cards.test.mjs`, `comments.test.mjs`, `db.test.mjs`, `server.test.mjs`) all fail immediately with:
150	
151	```
152	Error [ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND]: Cannot find package 'express' imported from .../server/index.js
153	Error [ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND]: Cannot find package 'better-sqlite3' imported from .../server/db/queries.js
154	```
155	
156	**Root cause:** The `kanban/server/node_modules/` directory does not exist — server dependencies (`express`, `cors`, `better-sqlite3`, `uuid`) are not installed. This is an environment/setup problem unrelated to task 12: the task only touched client-side files (`Board.jsx`, `Column.jsx`, `CreateCardForm.*`), and the server test failure is identical across all prior commits. `ws` was found in the workspace-hoisted `kanban/node_modules/` which is why `ws.test.mjs` ran (21 of its 25 tests pass; 4 failures appear to be pre-existing bugs in the ws test suite related to timing).
157	
158	---
159	
160	### 4.5 `npm run test:setup` — PASS
161	
162	**Exit code:** 0
163	
164	```
165	# tests 49
166	# pass 49
167	# fail 0
168	```
169	
170	All 49 setup/scaffolding tests pass (package.json structure, directory layout, vite.config proxy settings, etc.).
171	
172	---
173	
174	## 5. Code Review Findings
175	
176	### 5.1 Logic Note: `displayError` precedence means API errors can be masked by stale validation errors
177	
178	**File:** `kanban/client/src/components/CreateCardForm.jsx`, line 70
179	
180	```js
181	const displayError = validationError ?? apiError
182	```
183	
184	`??` (nullish coalescing) means that if `validationError` is set (non-null), `apiError` will never be shown. In the current implementation this cannot happen simultaneously because:
185	- Validation sets `validationError` and returns early (never calls `onSubmit`, so `apiError` stays null).
186	- A successful call to `onSubmit` clears both errors before starting.
187	
188	However, if the code is extended in the future (e.g., async title validation), a non-null `validationError` would silently shadow an `apiError`. The intent is clearly to prefer `validationError` over `apiError`, which works, but the dependency on two separate state variables (rather than a single `error` state) makes the precedence implicit and fragile. This is a minor design concern, not a current bug.
189	
190	### 5.2 Minor: `handleTitleChange` clears both error types on title input
191	
192	**File:** `CreateCardForm.jsx`, lines 47–49
193	
194	```js
195	if (validationError) setValidationError(null)
196	if (apiError) setApiError(null)
197	```
198	
199	Both are cleared together when the user types in the title field. This is correct behaviour and matches the test coverage (test: "clears API error when user starts typing in title after a failed submit"). No issue; noted for completeness.
200	
201	### 5.3 Minor Accessibility Gap: No `aria-describedby` on the title input
202	
203	The error paragraph uses `role="alert"`, which is ARIA-live by default and announces errors to screen readers when they appear. The title input does not have `aria-describedby` pointing at the error element. This is a minor accessibility gap — assistive technology users can hear the error announced when it appears but focus is not programmatically associated with the invalid field. Not a functional bug; noted as an improvement opportunity.
204	
205	### 5.4 Integration: `CreateCardForm` is only added to the "Ready" column — confirmed correct
206	
207	**File:** `Board.jsx`, lines 19–25
208	
209	The form is passed only as a `footer` to the "Ready" column. "In Progress" and "Done" columns do not receive a footer. This matches the specification and is confirmed by integration tests in `Board.test.jsx` (`does not render the "+ Add card" button in the In Progress column`, `does not render the "+ Add card" button in the Done column`).
210	
211	### 5.5 Pre-existing `act()` warnings in `useBoard.test.js` (not task 12)
212	
213	As noted in §4.1, four tests in `useBoard.test.js` emit `act(...)` warnings. These are not introduced by task 12 and should be addressed in a follow-up.
214	
215	---
216	
217	## 6. Overall Assessment
218	
219	**Task 12 implementation: PASS**
220	
221	The `CreateCardForm` component is correctly implemented and fully integrated. All client-facing commands (test, lint, build) pass with exit code 0. The 33 new unit tests and 4 new integration tests are comprehensive, covering: toggle behaviour, form field control, validation (empty/whitespace title), submission (trimming, null assignee for empty/whitespace), loading states, error display, Cancel button, and Escape key. The component is well-structured, follows existing code conventions, and introduces no regressions.
222	
223	**Outstanding issues not attributable to task 12:**
224	
225	| Issue | Severity | Origin |
226	|-------|----------|--------|
227	| `act(...)` warnings in `useBoard.test.js` | Low (tests still pass) | Prior task |
228	| Server tests fail (`express`/`better-sqlite3` not installed) | High (server test suite broken) | Environment / prior tasks |

| Requirement | Status | Notes |
|-------------|--------|-------|

| Handle empty state gracefully | PASS | `content` falsy → "No description" placeholder; cancel with null resets to empty paragraph |
| Add proper styling to match application theme | PASS (partial) | `BlockEditor.css` sets scoped layout and a colour override; overall theme alignment is minimal but present |

---

## 7. Code Review Findings

### 7.1 ISSUE: No explicit block type schema configured (headings, bullet lists, numbered lists, code blocks)

**Severity:** Medium

**File:** `kanban/client/src/components/CardModal/BlockEditor.jsx`

The task requirement explicitly states: _"Configure BlockNote with headings, paragraphs, bullet lists, numbered lists, and code blocks."_

The `BlockEditor` component calls:
```js
const editor = useCreateBlockNote({ initialContent: parseContent(content) })
```

No `schema` option is passed to `useCreateBlockNote`. BlockNote's default schema does include all the listed block types, so functionally the editor will support them via the slash command menu. However, the requirement calls for explicit configuration, which would typically be implemented by passing a custom schema to `useCreateBlockNote`:

```js
import { BlockNoteSchema, defaultBlockSpecs } from '@blocknote/core'

const schema = BlockNoteSchema.create({
  blockSpecs: {
    paragraph:         defaultBlockSpecs.paragraph,
    heading:           defaultBlockSpecs.heading,
    bulletListItem:    defaultBlockSpecs.bulletListItem,
    numberedListItem:  defaultBlockSpecs.numberedListItem,
    codeBlock:         defaultBlockSpecs.codeBlock,
  }
})
const editor = useCreateBlockNote({ schema, initialContent: parseContent(content) })
```

As implemented, the editor also makes other block types available (images, tables, files, etc.) that are not part of the requirements. The task spec implies a scoped/restricted editor configuration, but the implementation uses the unrestricted default. This is a partial compliance — the required block types work, but the editor is wider than specified.

---

### 7.2 ISSUE: `useCreateBlockNote` is called with a fixed `initialContent` — content prop changes are not reflected

**Severity:** Medium

**File:** `kanban/client/src/components/CardModal/BlockEditor.jsx`, line 21

```js
const editor = useCreateBlockNote({ initialContent: parseContent(content) })
```

`useCreateBlockNote` initialises the editor once on mount and ignores subsequent changes to the `initialContent` option. This is documented BlockNote behaviour: `initialContent` is an initialisation-only option. If the `content` prop changes (e.g., because another client updates the card via WebSocket and the parent passes a new `card.description`), the editor will silently display stale content.

The `handleCancel` function does correctly call `editor.replaceBlocks(editor.document, resetBlocks)` to manually reset the editor to the current `content` prop, so cancel scenarios work. But the component is not reactive to `content` prop changes from outside — if the parent re-renders with a new `content` value (real-time update scenario), the editor will not update unless it is unmounted and remounted.

Given that the application already supports WebSocket real-time updates, this is a realistic scenario that could cause a user to see and save stale description content.

---

### 7.3 Minor: `parseContent` fallback for plain text may produce incorrect BlockNote document structure

**Severity:** Low

**File:** `kanban/client/src/components/CardModal/BlockEditor.jsx`, lines 8–13

```js
export function parseContent(raw) {
  if (!raw) return undefined
  try {
    return JSON.parse(raw)
  } catch {
    return [{ type: 'paragraph', content: [{ type: 'text', text: raw, styles: {} }] }]
  }
}
```

When `raw` is a plain text string (e.g., legacy data), the fallback block is:
```js
{ type: 'paragraph', content: [{ type: 'text', text: raw, styles: {} }] }
```

A valid BlockNote block requires additional top-level properties (`id`, `props`). Without `id` and `props`, BlockNote may internally reject or silently autocorrect the block. The tests pass because `useCreateBlockNote` is mocked in tests and never exercises real BlockNote validation. In production, BlockNote's internal schema validation may log warnings or replace this block with an empty one.

This is a low risk in practice since any description saved via BlockNote will be stored as proper JSON going forward; only data that was never saved through BlockNote would trigger the fallback path.

---

### 7.4 Observation: Theme override uses a non-standard CSS selector

**Severity:** Low

**File:** `kanban/client/src/components/CardModal/BlockEditor.css`, lines 35–38

```css
.block-editor [data-theming-css-variables-demo] {
  --bn-colors-editor-text: #333;
}
```

The attribute selector `[data-theming-css-variables-demo]` targets a BlockNote element that appears to be an example/demo attribute name from the BlockNote documentation rather than a production-stable selector. If this attribute name changes in a future BlockNote version, the theme override will silently stop working. The CSS variable `--bn-colors-editor-text` itself is the right approach, but the selector could be more robust (e.g., `.bn-editor` or the root BlockNote container class).

---

### 7.5 Observation: `onEditingChange` is not guarded if `onSave` is not provided

**Severity:** Info (not a bug)

The `onEditingChange` prop is called with optional chaining (`onEditingChange?.(isEditing)`), making it optional. The `onSave` prop is used without optional chaining (`await onSave(...)`), so if `onSave` is not passed, clicking Save would throw a runtime error. However, this is effectively a required prop and all usages in `CardModal.jsx` pass it correctly.

---

### 7.6 Pre-existing: `act()` warnings in `useBoard.test.js`

As noted in prior QA reports, four tests in `useBoard.test.js` emit React `act(...)` warnings. Not introduced by this task.

---

## 8. Test Coverage Assessment

### `BlockEditor.test.jsx` — 29 tests in 4 groups

| Group | Tests | Coverage |
|-------|-------|----------|
| Basic rendering | 6 | Null/undefined/empty/falsy content, view-mode buttons, "No description" text |
| Initialization / parseContent | 3 | JSON, plain-text fallback, null → undefined initialContent |
| Edit/read-only mode toggle | 12 | Enter/exit edit mode, `editable` prop, Escape key, `onEditingChange` callback, cancel reset |
| Save / JSON serialisation | 8 | `onSave` called with `JSON.stringify`, loading state, error display, error clear on success |

Coverage is thorough for the implemented behaviour. No tests exercise the `parseContent` fallback in a real BlockNote environment (it is mocked), so the concern in §7.3 is not caught by the test suite.

### `CardModal.test.jsx` — 60 tests (5 new)

The 5 new `CardModal — description` tests verify:
- `BlockEditor` is rendered in place of a static paragraph
- `content` prop passes `card.description`
- `onSave` triggers `onUpdate(card.id, { description: json })`
- Escape does not close modal while `BlockEditor` reports `isEditing=true`
- Escape closes modal after description editing ends

All 5 tests pass. The `BlockEditor` is correctly mocked to isolate `CardModal` behaviour.

---

## 9. Overall Assessment

**Task 14 implementation: CONDITIONAL PASS**

The BlockNote editor is functionally integrated, all tests pass (362/362), lint is clean, and the build succeeds. The core requirements — wrapper component, edit/read-only toggle, JSON save to database, empty state handling, and styling — are all correctly implemented.

**Two issues warrant attention before this can be considered fully compliant:**

1. **No explicit block schema configuration (§7.1 — Medium):** The task explicitly requires configuring the editor with specific block types (headings, paragraphs, bullet lists, numbered lists, code blocks). The implementation relies on BlockNote's default schema instead, which includes all required types but also many others. This is a specification compliance gap, not a functional bug.

2. **Content prop changes not reflected in editor after mount (§7.2 — Medium):** Because `initialContent` is a one-time option, real-time description updates from other clients (delivered via WebSocket) will not update the editor's displayed content unless the component is unmounted and remounted. This is a functional limitation in the context of the app's real-time update architecture.

**Outstanding issues not attributable to task 14:**

| Issue | Severity | Origin |
|-------|----------|--------|
| `act(...)` warnings in `useBoard.test.js` | Low (tests still pass) | Prior task |
| Server tests fail (`express`/`better-sqlite3` not installed) | High (server test suite broken) | Environment / prior tasks |

212	
213	### 7.1 ISSUE: No explicit block type schema configured (headings, bullet lists, numbered lists, code blocks)
214	
215	**Severity:** Medium
216	
217	**File:** `kanban/client/src/components/CardModal/BlockEditor.jsx`
218	
219	The task requirement explicitly states: _"Configure BlockNote with headings, paragraphs, bullet lists, numbered lists, and code blocks."_
220	
221	The `BlockEditor` component calls:
222	```js
223	const editor = useCreateBlockNote({ initialContent: parseContent(content) })
224	```
225	
226	No `schema` option is passed to `useCreateBlockNote`. BlockNote's default schema does include all the listed block types, so functionally the editor will support them via the slash command menu. However, the requirement calls for explicit configuration, which would typically be implemented by passing a custom schema to `useCreateBlockNote`:
227	
228	```js
229	import { BlockNoteSchema, defaultBlockSpecs } from '@blocknote/core'
230	
231	const schema = BlockNoteSchema.create({
232	  blockSpecs: {
233	    paragraph:         defaultBlockSpecs.paragraph,
234	    heading:           defaultBlockSpecs.heading,
235	    bulletListItem:    defaultBlockSpecs.bulletListItem,
236	    numberedListItem:  defaultBlockSpecs.numberedListItem,
237	    codeBlock:         defaultBlockSpecs.codeBlock,
238	  }
239	})
240	const editor = useCreateBlockNote({ schema, initialContent: parseContent(content) })
241	```
242	
243	As implemented, the editor also makes other block types available (images, tables, files, etc.) that are not part of the requirements. The task spec implies a scoped/restricted editor configuration, but the implementation uses the unrestricted default. This is a partial compliance — the required block types work, but the editor is wider than specified.
244	
245	---
246	
247	### 7.2 ISSUE: `useCreateBlockNote` is called with a fixed `initialContent` — content prop changes are not reflected
248	
249	**Severity:** Medium
250	
251	**File:** `kanban/client/src/components/CardModal/BlockEditor.jsx`, line 21
252	
253	```js
254	const editor = useCreateBlockNote({ initialContent: parseContent(content) })
255	```
256	
257	`useCreateBlockNote` initialises the editor once on mount and ignores subsequent changes to the `initialContent` option. This is documented BlockNote behaviour: `initialContent` is an initialisation-only option. If the `content` prop changes (e.g., because another client updates the card via WebSocket and the parent passes a new `card.description`), the editor will silently display stale content.
258	
259	The `handleCancel` function does correctly call `editor.replaceBlocks(editor.document, resetBlocks)` to manually reset the editor to the current `content` prop, so cancel scenarios work. But the component is not reactive to `content` prop changes from outside — if the parent re-renders with a new `content` value (real-time update scenario), the editor will not update unless it is unmounted and remounted.
260	
261	Given that the application already supports WebSocket real-time updates, this is a realistic scenario that could cause a user to see and save stale description content.

266	
267	**Severity:** Low
268	
269	**File:** `kanban/client/src/components/CardModal/BlockEditor.jsx`, lines 8–13
270	
271	```js

273	  if (!raw) return undefined
274	  try {
275	    return JSON.parse(raw)
276	  } catch {
277	    return [{ type: 'paragraph', content: [{ type: 'text', text: raw, styles: {} }] }]
278	  }
279	}
280	```
281	
282	When `raw` is a plain text string (e.g., legacy data), the fallback block is:
283	```js
284	{ type: 'paragraph', content: [{ type: 'text', text: raw, styles: {} }] }
285	```
286	
287	A valid BlockNote block requires additional top-level properties (`id`, `props`). Without `id` and `props`, BlockNote may internally reject or silently autocorrect the block. The tests pass because `useCreateBlockNote` is mocked in tests and never exercises real BlockNote validation. In production, BlockNote's internal schema validation may log warnings or replace this block with an empty one.
288	
289	This is a low risk in practice since any description saved via BlockNote will be stored as proper JSON going forward; only data that was never saved through BlockNote would trigger the fallback path.
290	
291	---

293	### 7.4 Observation: Theme override uses a non-standard CSS selector
294	
295	**Severity:** Low
296	
297	**File:** `kanban/client/src/components/CardModal/BlockEditor.css`, lines 35–38
298

302	}
303	```
304	
305	The attribute selector `[data-theming-css-variables-demo]` targets a BlockNote element that appears to be an example/demo attribute name from the BlockNote documentation rather than a production-stable selector. If this attribute name changes in a future BlockNote version, the theme override will silently stop working. The CSS variable `--bn-colors-editor-text` itself is the right approach, but the selector could be more robust (e.g., `.bn-editor` or the root BlockNote container class).
306	
307	---
308	
309	### 7.5 Observation: `onEditingChange` is not guarded if `onSave` is not provided
310	
311	**Severity:** Info (not a bug)
312	
313	The `onEditingChange` prop is called with optional chaining (`onEditingChange?.(isEditing)`), making it optional. The `onSave` prop is used without optional chaining (`await onSave(...)`), so if `onSave` is not passed, clicking Save would throw a runtime error. However, this is effectively a required prop and all usages in `CardModal.jsx` pass it correctly.
314	
315	---

317	### 7.6 Pre-existing: `act()` warnings in `useBoard.test.js`
318	
319	As noted in prior QA reports, four tests in `useBoard.test.js` emit React `act(...)` warnings. Not introduced by this task.

327	| Group | Tests | Coverage |
328	|-------|-------|----------|
329	| Basic rendering | 6 | Null/undefined/empty/falsy content, view-mode buttons, "No description" text |
330	| Initialization / parseContent | 3 | JSON, plain-text fallback, null → undefined initialContent |
331	| Edit/read-only mode toggle | 12 | Enter/exit edit mode, `editable` prop, Escape key, `onEditingChange` callback, cancel reset |
332	| Save / JSON serialisation | 8 | `onSave` called with `JSON.stringify`, loading state, error display, error clear on success |

342	- Escape does not close modal while `BlockEditor` reports `isEditing=true`
343	- Escape closes modal after description editing ends

345	All 5 tests pass. The `BlockEditor` is correctly mocked to isolate `CardModal` behaviour.

352	
353	The BlockNote editor is functionally integrated, all tests pass (362/362), lint is clean, and the build succeeds. The core requirements — wrapper component, edit/read-only toggle, JSON save to database, empty state handling, and styling — are all correctly implemented.

The implementation relies on BlockNote's default schema instead, which includes all required types but also many others. This is a specification compliance gap, not a functional bug.

## 7. Code Review Findings

### 7.1 ISSUE: No explicit block type schema configured (headings, bullet lists, numbered lists, code blocks)

**Severity:** Medium

**File:** `kanban/client/src/components/CardModal/BlockEditor.jsx`

The task requirement explicitly states: _"Configure BlockNote with headings, paragraphs, bullet lists, numbered lists, and code blocks."_

The `BlockEditor` component calls:
```js
const editor = useCreateBlockNote({ initialContent: parseContent(content) })
```

No `schema` option is passed to `useCreateBlockNote`. BlockNote's default schema does include all the listed block types, so functionally the editor will support them via the slash command menu. However, the requirement calls for explicit configuration, which would typically be implemented by passing a custom schema to `useCreateBlockNote`:

```js
import { BlockNoteSchema, defaultBlockSpecs } from '@blocknote/core'

const schema = BlockNoteSchema.create({
  blockSpecs: {
    paragraph:         defaultBlockSpecs.paragraph,
    heading:           defaultBlockSpecs.heading,
    bulletListItem:    defaultBlockSpecs.bulletListItem,
    numberedListItem:  defaultBlockSpecs.numberedListItem,
    codeBlock:         defaultBlockSpecs.codeBlock,
  }
})
const editor = useCreateBlockNote({ schema, initialContent: parseContent(content) })
```

As implemented, the editor also makes other block types available (images, tables, files, etc.) that are not part of the requirements. The task spec implies a scoped/restricted editor configuration, but the implementation uses the unrestricted default. This is a partial compliance — the required block types work, but the editor is wider than specified.

---

### 7.2 ISSUE: `useCreateBlockNote` is called with a fixed `initialContent` — content prop changes are not reflected

**Severity:** Medium

**File:** `kanban/client/src/components/CardModal/BlockEditor.jsx`, line 21

```js
const editor = useCreateBlockNote({ initialContent: parseContent(content) })
```

`useCreateBlockNote` initialises the editor once on mount and ignores subsequent changes to the `initialContent` option. This is documented BlockNote behaviour: `initialContent` is an initialisation-only option. If the `content` prop changes (e.g., because another client updates the card via WebSocket and the parent passes a new `card.description`), the editor will silently display stale content.

The `handleCancel` function does correctly call `editor.replaceBlocks(editor.document, resetBlocks)` to manually reset the editor to the current `content` prop, so cancel scenarios work. But the component is not reactive to `content` prop changes from outside — if the parent re-renders with a new `content` value (real-time update scenario), the editor will not update unless it is unmounted and remounted.

Given that the application already supports WebSocket real-time updates, this is a realistic scenario that could cause a user to see and save stale description content.

---

### 7.3 Minor: `parseContent` fallback for plain text may produce incorrect BlockNote document structure

**Severity:** Low

**File:** `kanban/client/src/components/CardModal/BlockEditor.jsx`, lines 8–13

```js
export function parseContent(raw) {
  if (!raw) return undefined
  try {
    return JSON.parse(raw)
  } catch {
    return [{ type: 'paragraph', content: [{ type: 'text', text: raw, styles: {} }] }]
  }
}
```

When `raw` is a plain text string (e.g., legacy data), the fallback block is:
```js
{ type: 'paragraph', content: [{ type: 'text', text: raw, styles: {} }] }
```

A valid BlockNote block requires additional top-level properties (`id`, `props`). Without `id` and `props`, BlockNote may internally reject or silently autocorrect the block. The tests pass because `useCreateBlockNote` is mocked in tests and never exercises real BlockNote validation. In production, BlockNote's internal schema validation may log warnings or replace this block with an empty one.

This is a low risk in practice since any description saved via BlockNote will be stored as proper JSON going forward; only data that was never saved through BlockNote would trigger the fallback path.

---

**Severity:** Low

**File:** `kanban/client/src/components/CardModal/BlockEditor.css`, lines 35–38

```css
.block-editor [data-theming-css-variables-demo] {
  --bn-colors-editor-text: #333;
}
```

The attribute selector `[data-theming-css-variables-demo]` targets a BlockNote element that appears to be an example/demo attribute name from the BlockNote documentation rather than a production-stable selector. If this attribute name changes in a future BlockNote version, the theme override will silently stop working. The CSS variable `--bn-colors-editor-text` itself is the right approach, but the selector could be more robust (e.g., `.bn-editor` or the root BlockNote container class).

---

### 7.5 Observation: `onEditingChange` is not guarded if `onSave` is not provided

**Severity:** Info (not a bug)

The `onEditingChange` prop is called with optional chaining (`onEditingChange?.(isEditing)`), making it optional. The `onSave` prop is used without optional chaining (`await onSave(...)`), so if `onSave` is not passed, clicking Save would throw a runtime error. However, this is effectively a required prop and all usages in `CardModal.jsx` pass it correctly.

---

### 7.6 Pre-existing: `act()` warnings in `useBoard.test.js`

As noted in prior QA reports, four tests in `useBoard.test.js` emit React `act(...)` warnings. Not introduced by this task.

---

## 8. Test Coverage Assessment

### `BlockEditor.test.jsx` — 29 tests in 4 groups

| Group | Tests | Coverage |
|-------|-------|----------|
| Basic rendering | 6 | Null/undefined/empty/falsy content, view-mode buttons, "No description" text |
| Initialization / parseContent | 3 | JSON, plain-text fallback, null → undefined initialContent |
| Edit/read-only mode toggle | 12 | Enter/exit edit mode, `editable` prop, Escape key, `onEditingChange` callback, cancel reset |
| Save / JSON serialisation | 8 | `onSave` called with `JSON.stringify`, loading state, error display, error clear on success |

Coverage is thorough for the implemented behaviour. No tests exercise the `parseContent` fallback in a real BlockNote environment (it is mocked), so the concern in §7.3 is not caught by the test suite.

### `CardModal.test.jsx` — 60 tests (5 new)

The 5 new `CardModal — description` tests verify:
- `BlockEditor` is rendered in place of a static paragraph
- `content` prop passes `card.description`
- `onSave` triggers `onUpdate(card.id, { description: json })`
- Escape does not close modal while `BlockEditor` reports `isEditing=true`
- Escape closes modal after description editing ends

All 5 tests pass. The `BlockEditor` is correctly mocked to isolate `CardModal` behaviour.

---

## 9. Overall Assessment

**Task 14 implementation: CONDITIONAL PASS**

The BlockNote editor is functionally integrated, all tests pass (362/362), lint is clean, and the build succeeds. The core requirements — wrapper component, edit/read-only toggle, JSON save to database, empty state handling, and styling — are all correctly implemented.

**Two issues warrant attention before this can be considered fully compliant:**

1. **No explicit block schema configuration (§7.1 — Medium):** The task explicitly requires configuring the editor with specific block types (headings, paragraphs, bullet lists, numbered lists, code blocks). The implementation relies on BlockNote's default schema instead, which includes all required types but also many others. This is a specification compliance gap, not a functional bug.

2. **Content prop changes not reflected in editor after mount (§7.2 — Medium):** Because `initialContent` is a one-time option, real-time description updates from other clients (delivered via WebSocket) will not update the editor's displayed content unless the component is unmounted and remounted. This is a functional limitation in the context of the app's real-time update architecture.

**Outstanding issues not attributable to task 14:**

| Issue | Severity | Origin |
|-------|----------|--------|
| `act(...)` warnings in `useBoard.test.js` | Low (tests still pass) | Prior task |
| Server tests fail (`express`/`better-sqlite3` not installed) | High (server test suite broken) | Environment / prior tasks |

# QA Report — Task 14: Integrate BlockNote Rich Text Editor

**Date:** 2026-04-10  
**Worktree:** `/Users/benjamin/.sofactory/worktrees/kanban-board/kanban-board-14`  
**Commit:** `4b67355` — _feat(kanban-board): task 14 - Integrate BlockNote rich text editor_  
**Reviewer role:** QA Engineer (read-only, no fixes applied)

---

## 1. Project Structure Overview

```
kanban-board-14/
└── kanban/
    ├── package.json               # Workspace root (client + server workspaces)
    ├── test/
    │   ├── setup.test.mjs         # Root structural tests (49 tests)
    │   └── client.setup.test.mjs  # Client dependency/file tests
    ├── client/
    │   ├── package.json           # Vitest, ESLint, Vite scripts
    │   ├── eslint.config.js
    │   ├── vite.config.js
    │   └── src/
    │       └── components/
    │           ├── Board/
    │           │   └── CardModal.jsx / CardModal.test.jsx  ← modified
    │           └── CardModal/
    │               ├── BlockEditor.jsx        ← NEW
    │               ├── BlockEditor.css        ← NEW
    │               └── BlockEditor.test.jsx   ← NEW
    └── server/
        ├── package.json
        └── (node_modules NOT installed in worktree)
```

### Files changed in this task (from `git diff HEAD~1 --name-only`):

| File | Status |
|------|--------|
| `kanban/client/package.json` | Modified — added `@blocknote/core` and `@blocknote/react` |
| `kanban/client/src/components/Board/CardModal.jsx` | Modified — integrated BlockEditor |
| `kanban/client/src/components/Board/CardModal.test.jsx` | Modified — added description test suite |
| `kanban/client/src/components/CardModal/BlockEditor.css` | New file |
| `kankan/client/src/components/CardModal/BlockEditor.jsx` | New file |
| `kanban/client/src/components/CardModal/BlockEditor.test.jsx` | New file |
| `kanban/package-lock.json` | Modified — added BlockNote lock entries |

---

## 2. Commands Found

| # | Command | Source |
|---|---------|--------|
| 1 | `npm -w client run test` | `client/package.json` → `scripts.test` (Vitest) |
| 2 | `npm -w client run lint` | `client/package.json` → `scripts.lint` (ESLint) |
| 3 | `npm -w client run build` | `client/package.json` → `scripts.build` (Vite) |
| 4 | `npm -w server run test` | Root `package.json` → `scripts.test:server` |
| 5 | `node --test test/*.test.mjs` | Root `package.json` → `scripts.test:setup` |

No `Makefile` found. No TypeScript typecheck script (project uses plain JS/JSX, no `tsconfig.json`).

---

## 3. Command Results

### 3.1 `npm -w client run test` — ✅ PASS

**Exit code:** 0

```
 Test Files  11 passed (11)
      Tests  365 passed (365)
   Start at  18:18:49
   Duration  5.75s
```

All 365 tests pass across 11 test files. Task-14-relevant results:

| File | Tests | Result |
|------|-------|--------|
| `src/components/CardModal/BlockEditor.test.jsx` | 32 | ✅ All pass |
| `src/components/Board/CardModal.test.jsx` | 60 | ✅ All pass (5 new description tests included) |

**Warnings (pre-existing, not introduced by this task):**  
Four tests in `src/hooks/useBoard.test.js` emit React `act(...)` warnings to stderr. These do not cause test failures and originate from code unchanged by this task.

```
Warning: An update to TestComponent inside a test was not wrapped in act(...)
  > WebSocket integration — initialization > calls useWebSocket with a ws:// URL containing /ws
  > WebSocket integration — initialization > subscribes to all 5 event types
  > WebSocket integration — initialization > passes an onEvent callback to useWebSocket
  > multi-client state consistency > refetches board state when WebSocket reconnects after disconnect
```

---

### 3.2 `npm -w client run lint` — ✅ PASS

**Exit code:** 0  
No ESLint errors or warnings. The `BlockEditor.jsx` uses one intentional `// eslint-disable-next-line react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` on the Escape-key `useEffect`, which is acceptable given the stable semantics of `handleCancel`.

---

### 3.3 `npm -w client run build` — ✅ PASS (with expected warning)

**Exit code:** 0

```
vite v5.4.21 building for production...
✓ 558 modules transformed.
dist/index.html                     0.48 kB │ gzip:   0.31 kB
dist/assets/index-BZa6x_DO.css     29.84 kB │ gzip:   6.03 kB
dist/assets/module-BvCTiNll.js     77.23 kB │ gzip:  27.78 kB
dist/assets/native-B5Vb9Oiz.js    380.35 kB │ gzip:  82.06 kB
dist/assets/index-dV7-MwWi.js   1,354.66 kB │ gzip: 418.79 kB

(!) Some chunks are larger than 500 kB after minification.
✓ built in 1.44s
```

The large-chunk warning (~1.35 MB unminified) is expected: BlockNote bundles ProseMirror, Tiptap, and related dependencies. The build exits cleanly with code 0. No code-splitting of the editor is required by the task spec.

---

### 3.4 `npm -w server run test` — ❌ FAIL (pre-existing environment issue)

**Exit code:** 1

```
# tests 25  (only ws.test.mjs ran; other test files fail to load)
# pass 21
# fail 4
```

Four test files (`cards.test.mjs`, `comments.test.mjs`, `db.test.mjs`, `server.test.mjs`) immediately throw:

```
Error [ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND]: Cannot find package 'express' imported from .../server/index.js
Error [ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND]: Cannot find package 'better-sqlite3' imported from .../server/db/queries.js
```

**Root cause:** `kanban/server/node_modules/` does not exist in the worktree (confirmed by `ls` returning "No such file or directory"). Server dependencies are not installed. This failure is identical across all prior task branches — it is a worktree environment setup issue, not introduced by Task 14. Task 14 only touches client-side files; no server code was modified.

---

### 3.5 `node --test test/*.test.mjs` — ✅ PASS

**Exit code:** 0

```
# tests 49
# suites 9
# pass 49
# fail 0
# duration_ms 45.148541
```

All 49 structural/scaffold tests pass (workspace layout, `package.json` shape, Vite proxy config, etc.).

---

## 4. Dependency Verification

| Package | Required by task | `client/package.json` entry | Installed version |
|---------|------------------|-----------------------------|-------------------|
| `@blocknote/react` | ✅ Yes | `^0.47.3` (dependencies) | 0.47.3 ✅ |
| `@blocknote/core` | ✅ Yes | `^0.47.3` (dependencies) | 0.47.3 ✅ |

Both packages are listed as runtime `dependencies` (not `devDependencies`), which is correct since they are included in the production bundle. They are hoisted to the workspace root `node_modules/@blocknote/`.

---

## 5. Feature Checklist Against Task Requirements

| Requirement | Status | Notes |
|-------------|--------|-------|
| Install `@blocknote/react` and `@blocknote/core` | ✅ PASS | Both at 0.47.3, listed as production deps |
| Create `client/src/components/CardModal/BlockEditor.jsx` | ✅ PASS | File exists, 114 lines, well-structured |
| Configure BlockNote with headings, paragraphs, bullet lists, numbered lists, and code blocks | ✅ PASS | Explicit `BlockNoteSchema.create` with exactly 5 block specs: `paragraph`, `heading`, `bulletListItem`, `numberedListItem`, `codeBlock` |
| Add edit/read-only mode toggle | ✅ PASS | `isEditing` state drives `editable` prop; Save/Cancel buttons in edit mode, Edit button in view mode; `onEditingChange` callback fires correctly |
| Save description as JSON string to database | ✅ PASS | `JSON.stringify(editor.document)` passed to `onSave`; CardModal calls `onUpdate(card.id, { description: json })` |
| Handle empty state gracefully | ✅ PASS | `content` falsy → "No description" placeholder; cancel with null resets to empty paragraph block |
| Add proper styling to match application theme | ✅ PASS | `BlockEditor.css` provides scoped layout and a CSS variable override (`.block-editor .bn-editor { --bn-colors-editor-text: #333; }`) |
| Integration with `CardModal` | ✅ PASS | `<BlockEditor>` replaces static description field; `isEditingDescription` prevents modal Escape-close during editor editing |

---

## 6. Code Review Findings

### 6.1 Observation: `parseContent` plain-text fallback produces a block without `id` and `props`

**Severity:** Low  
**File:** `BlockEditor.jsx`, line 25

```js
return [{ type: 'paragraph', content: [{ type: 'text', text: raw, styles: {} }] }]
```

A fully valid BlockNote block requires `id` and `props` at the top level. The fallback is only triggered when `raw` is not parseable JSON (e.g., legacy plain-text descriptions). In practice, any description written through BlockNote is stored as proper JSON, so this path is only hit for pre-existing data. BlockNote may internally emit warnings or auto-correct the block. This is low risk.

---

### 6.2 Observation: `onSave` is not guarded with optional chaining

**Severity:** Info (not a bug in practice)  
**File:** `BlockEditor.jsx`, line 77

```js
await onSave(JSON.stringify(editor.document))
```

`onSave` is called without optional chaining, making it a de-facto required prop. All usages in `CardModal.jsx` pass it correctly. The concern is only if the component is reused elsewhere without `onSave` — it would throw a runtime error. Consistent with how `onSave` is treated as required.

---

### 6.3 Pre-existing: `act()` warnings in `useBoard.test.js`

**Severity:** Low (tests still pass)  
**Origin:** Carried over from prior tasks; not related to Task 14.

---

-### 7.1 ISSUE: No explicit block type schema configured (headings, bullet lists, numbered lists, code blocks)
+### 6.1 Observation: `parseContent` plain-text fallback produces a block without `id` and `props`
-The task requirement explicitly states: _"Configure BlockNote with headings, paragraphs, bullet lists, numbered lists, and code blocks."_
+return [{ type: 'paragraph', content: [{ type: 'text', text: raw, styles: {} }] }]

-No `schema` option is passed to `useCreateBlockNote`. BlockNote's default schema does include all the listed block types, so functionally the editor will support them via the slash command menu. However, the requirement calls for explicit configuration, which would typically be implemented by passing a custom schema to `useCreateBlockNote`:

+A fully valid BlockNote block requires `id` and `props` at the top level. The fallback is only triggered when `raw` is not parseable JSON (e.g., legacy plain-text descriptions). In practice, any description written through BlockNote is stored as proper JSON, so this path is only hit for pre-existing data. BlockNote may internally emit warnings or auto-correct the block. This is low risk.

-As implemented, the editor also makes other block types available (images, tables, files, etc.) that are not part of the requirements. The task spec implies a scoped/restricted editor configuration, but the implementation uses the unrestricted default. This is a partial compliance — the required block types work, but the editor is wider than specified.
-### 7.2 ISSUE: `useCreateBlockNote` is called with a fixed `initialContent` — content prop changes are not reflected
+### 6.2 Observation: `onSave` is not guarded with optional chaining

-`useCreateBlockNote` initialises the editor once on mount and ignores subsequent changes to the `initialContent` option. This is documented BlockNote behaviour: `initialContent` is an initialisation-only option. If the `content` prop changes (e.g., because another client updates the card via WebSocket and the parent passes a new `card.description`), the editor will silently display stale content.

+`onSave` is called without optional chaining, making it a de-facto required prop. All usages in `CardModal.jsx` pass it correctly. The concern is only if the component is reused elsewhere without `onSave` — it would throw a runtime error. Consistent with how `onSave` is treated as required.

-The `handleCancel` function does correctly call `editor.replaceBlocks(editor.document, resetBlocks)` to manually reset the editor to the current `content` prop, so cancel scenarios work. But the component is not reactive to `content` prop changes from outside — if the parent re-renders with a new `content` value (real-time update scenario), the editor will not update unless it is unmounted and remounted.
-Given that the application already supports WebSocket real-time updates, this is a realistic scenario that could cause a user to see and save stale description content.
-### 7.3 Minor: `parseContent` fallback for plain text may produce incorrect BlockNote document structure
+**Origin:** Carried over from prior tasks; not related to Task 14.
-    return [{ type: 'paragraph', content: [{ type: 'text', text: raw, styles: {} }] }]
-When `raw` is a plain text string (e.g., legacy data), the fallback block is:
-{ type: 'paragraph', content: [{ type: 'text', text: raw, styles: {} }] }

-A valid BlockNote block requires additional top-level properties (`id`, `props`). Without `id` and `props`, BlockNote may internally reject or silently autocorrect the block. The tests pass because `useCreateBlockNote` is mocked in tests and never exercises real BlockNote validation. In production, BlockNote's internal schema validation may log warnings or replace this block with an empty one.

-This is a low risk in practice since any description saved via BlockNote will be stored as proper JSON going forward; only data that was never saved through BlockNote would trigger the fallback path.
-### 7.4 Observation: Theme override uses a non-standard CSS selector
+### 6.4 Pre-existing: Server tests fail due to missing `node_modules`
+**Severity:** High (server test suite broken), but **not introduced by this task**
+**Origin:** Worktree environment — `npm install` was not run for the server workspace in this worktree.

-The attribute selector `[data-theming-css-variables-demo]` targets a BlockNote element that appears to be an example/demo attribute name from the BlockNote documentation rather than a production-stable selector. If this attribute name changes in a future BlockNote version, the theme override will silently stop working. The CSS variable `--bn-colors-editor-text` itself is the right approach, but the selector could be more robust (e.g., `.bn-editor` or the root BlockNote container class).
-### 7.5 Observation: `onEditingChange` is not guarded if `onSave` is not provided

-The `onEditingChange` prop is called with optional chaining (`onEditingChange?.(isEditing)`), making it optional. The `onSave` prop is used without optional chaining (`await onSave(...)`), so if `onSave` is not passed, clicking Save would throw a runtime error. However, this is effectively a required prop and all usages in `CardModal.jsx` pass it correctly.
-As noted in prior QA reports, four tests in `useBoard.test.js` emit React `act(...)` warnings. Not introduced by this task.
-| Basic rendering | 6 | Null/undefined/empty/falsy content, view-mode buttons, "No description" text |
-| Initialization / parseContent | 3 | JSON, plain-text fallback, null → undefined initialContent |
-| Edit/read-only mode toggle | 12 | Enter/exit edit mode, `editable` prop, Escape key, `onEditingChange` callback, cancel reset |
-| Save / JSON serialisation | 8 | `onSave` called with `JSON.stringify`, loading state, error display, error clear on success |
+| Basic rendering | 6 | Null/undefined/empty content renders "No description"; provided content renders BlockNoteViewRaw; Edit button present |
+| Initialization | 4 | JSON parse, plain-text fallback, null → `undefined` initialContent; explicit `BlockNoteSchema` with exactly 5 block types and correct names |

+| Mode toggle | 14 | Enter/exit edit mode, `editable` prop, Escape key, `onEditingChange` callback, cancel reset, external content prop reactivity (replaceBlocks called on prop change, but not during active edit) |
+| Save | 8 | `onSave` called with `JSON.stringify`, loading state disables buttons, error display on rejection, error clears on success |

-Coverage is thorough for the implemented behaviour. No tests exercise the `parseContent` fallback in a real BlockNote environment (it is mocked), so the concern in §7.3 is not caught by the test suite.

+Coverage is thorough. The schema validation test (group 2, test 4) explicitly verifies that `BlockNoteSchema` is passed and contains exactly the 5 required block types — satisfying Subtask 2's specification.
-- `BlockEditor` is rendered in place of a static paragraph
-- Escape does not close modal while `BlockEditor` reports `isEditing=true`
-- Escape closes modal after description editing ends
+| Escape guarded while editing | `onClose` not called when `isEditingDescription=true` |
+| Escape closes after editing ends | `onClose` called once after cancel |
-All 5 tests pass. The `BlockEditor` is correctly mocked to isolate `CardModal` behaviour.

-The BlockNote editor is functionally integrated, all tests pass (362/362), lint is clean, and the build succeeds. The core requirements — wrapper component, edit/read-only toggle, JSON save to database, empty state handling, and styling — are all correctly implemented.
+All client tests pass (365/365), lint is clean, and the production build succeeds. Every task requirement has been implemented correctly:
-**Two issues warrant attention before this can be considered fully compliant:**
+- `BlockEditor.jsx` wrapper component created with proper React hooks
+- **Explicit block schema** configured with exactly the 5 required block types (paragraph, heading, bulletListItem, numberedListItem, codeBlock) — no extra block types exposed
+- Edit/read-only toggle implemented via `isEditing` state and `editable` prop
+- Empty state ("No description" placeholder) handled gracefully
+- Styling integrated via scoped CSS with BlockNote CSS variable override
+- `CardModal` integration complete with Escape-key guard during description editing
+- External content reactivity implemented (replaceBlocks called when `content` prop changes while not editing)

-1. **No explicit block schema configuration (§7.1 — Medium):** The task explicitly requires configuring the editor with specific block types (headings, paragraphs, bullet lists, numbered lists, code blocks). The implementation relies on BlockNote's default schema instead, which includes all required types but also many others. This is a specification compliance gap, not a functional bug.

-2. **Content prop changes not reflected in editor after mount (§7.2 — Medium):** Because `initialContent` is a one-time option, real-time description updates from other clients (delivered via WebSocket) will not update the editor's displayed content unless the component is unmounted and remounted. This is a functional limitation in the context of the app's real-time update architecture.
-| `act(...)` warnings in `useBoard.test.js` | Low (tests still pass) | Prior task |
-| Server tests fail (`express`/`better-sqlite3` not installed) | High (server test suite broken) | Environment / prior tasks |

+The only notable issues are low-severity (plain-text fallback block missing `id`/`props` fields) and pre-existing (server `node_modules` missing, `act()` warnings in unrelated tests). Neither was introduced by this task.

## 3. Commands Found and Executed

---

### 4.4 `npm -w server run test` — FAIL (pre-existing environment issue)

**Exit code:** 1

```

```

---

### 4.5 `node --test test/*.test.mjs` — PASS

**Exit code:** 0

```

# pass 49

```

## 5. Dependency Verification

| Package | Required | Listed in `client/package.json` | Installed version |
|---------|----------|---------------------------------|-------------------|
| `@blocknote/react` | Yes | `^0.47.3` (dependencies) | 0.47.3 ✓ |
| `@blocknote/core` | Yes | `^0.47.3` (dependencies) | 0.47.3 ✓ |

Both packages are listed as runtime `dependencies` (not `devDependencies`), which is correct given that the component is part of the production bundle.

---

## 6. Feature Checklist Against Task Requirements

| Requirement | Status | Notes |
|-------------|--------|-------|
| Install `@blocknote/react` and `@blocknote/core` | PASS | Both at 0.47.3, listed as production deps |
| Create `client/src/components/CardModal/BlockEditor.jsx` wrapper | PASS | File exists and is well-structured |
| Configure BlockNote with headings, paragraphs, bullet lists, numbered lists, and code blocks | **PARTIAL — see §7.1** | No explicit block schema is configured; the default schema is used |
| Add edit/read-only mode toggle | PASS | `isEditing` state drives `editable` prop on `BlockNoteViewRaw` |
| Save description as JSON string to database | PASS | `JSON.stringify(editor.document)` passed to `onSave`; server accepts `description` TEXT field |
| Handle empty state gracefully | PASS | `content` falsy → "No description" placeholder; cancel with null resets to empty paragraph |
| Add proper styling to match application theme | PASS (partial) | `BlockEditor.css` sets scoped layout and a colour override; overall theme alignment is minimal but present |

---

## 7. Code Review Findings

### 7.1 ISSUE: No explicit block type schema configured (headings, bullet lists, numbered lists, code blocks)

**Severity:** Medium

**File:** `kanban/client/src/components/CardModal/BlockEditor.jsx`

The task requirement explicitly states: _"Configure BlockNote with headings, paragraphs, bullet lists, numbered lists, and code blocks."_

The `BlockEditor` component calls:
```js
const editor = useCreateBlockNote({ initialContent: parseContent(content) })
```

No `schema` option is passed to `useCreateBlockNote`. BlockNote's default schema does include all the listed block types, so functionally the editor will support them via the slash command menu. However, the requirement calls for explicit configuration, which would typically be implemented by passing a custom schema to `useCreateBlockNote`:

```js
import { BlockNoteSchema, defaultBlockSpecs } from '@blocknote/core'

const schema = BlockNoteSchema.create({
  blockSpecs: {
    paragraph:         defaultBlockSpecs.paragraph,
    heading:           defaultBlockSpecs.heading,
    bulletListItem:    defaultBlockSpecs.bulletListItem,
    numberedListItem:  defaultBlockSpecs.numberedListItem,
    codeBlock:         defaultBlockSpecs.codeBlock,
  }
})
const editor = useCreateBlockNote({ schema, initialContent: parseContent(content) })
```

As implemented, the editor also makes other block types available (images, tables, files, etc.) that are not part of the requirements. The task spec implies a scoped/restricted editor configuration, but the implementation uses the unrestricted default. This is a partial compliance — the required block types work, but the editor is wider than specified.

---

### 7.2 ISSUE: `useCreateBlockNote` is called with a fixed `initialContent` — content prop changes are not reflected

**Severity:** Medium

**File:** `kanban/client/src/components/CardModal/BlockEditor.jsx`, line 21

```js
const editor = useCreateBlockNote({ initialContent: parseContent(content) })
```

`useCreateBlockNote` initialises the editor once on mount and ignores subsequent changes to the `initialContent` option. This is documented BlockNote behaviour: `initialContent` is an initialisation-only option. If the `content` prop changes (e.g., because another client updates the card via WebSocket and the parent passes a new `card.description`), the editor will silently display stale content.

The `handleCancel` function does correctly call `editor.replaceBlocks(editor.document, resetBlocks)` to manually reset the editor to the current `content` prop, so cancel scenarios work. But the component is not reactive to `content` prop changes from outside — if the parent re-renders with a new `content` value (real-time update scenario), the editor will not update unless it is unmounted and remounted.

Given that the application already supports WebSocket real-time updates, this is a realistic scenario that could cause a user to see and save stale description content.

---

### 7.3 Minor: `parseContent` fallback for plain text may produce incorrect BlockNote document structure

**Severity:** Low

**File:** `kanban/client/src/components/CardModal/BlockEditor.jsx`, lines 8–13

```js
export function parseContent(raw) {
  if (!raw) return undefined
  try {
    return JSON.parse(raw)
  } catch {
    return [{ type: 'paragraph', content: [{ type: 'text', text: raw, styles: {} }] }]
  }
}
```

When `raw` is a plain text string (e.g., legacy data), the fallback block is:
```js
{ type: 'paragraph', content: [{ type: 'text', text: raw, styles: {} }] }
```

A valid BlockNote block requires additional top-level properties (`id`, `props`). Without `id` and `props`, BlockNote may internally reject or silently autocorrect the block. The tests pass because `useCreateBlockNote` is mocked in tests and never exercises real BlockNote validation. In production, BlockNote's internal schema validation may log warnings or replace this block with an empty one.

This is a low risk in practice since any description saved via BlockNote will be stored as proper JSON going forward; only data that was never saved through BlockNote would trigger the fallback path.

---

### 7.4 Observation: Theme override uses a non-standard CSS selector

**Severity:** Low

**File:** `kanban/client/src/components/CardModal/BlockEditor.css`, lines 35–38

```css
.block-editor [data-theming-css-variables-demo] {
  --bn-colors-editor-text: #333;
}
```

The attribute selector `[data-theming-css-variables-demo]` targets a BlockNote element that appears to be an example/demo attribute name from the BlockNote documentation rather than a production-stable selector. If this attribute name changes in a future BlockNote version, the theme override will silently stop working. The CSS variable `--bn-colors-editor-text` itself is the right approach, but the selector could be more robust (e.g., `.bn-editor` or the root BlockNote container class).

---

### 7.5 Observation: `onEditingChange` is not guarded if `onSave` is not provided

**Severity:** Info (not a bug)

The `onEditingChange` prop is called with optional chaining (`onEditingChange?.(isEditing)`), making it optional. The `onSave` prop is used without optional chaining (`await onSave(...)`), so if `onSave` is not passed, clicking Save would throw a runtime error. However, this is effectively a required prop and all usages in `CardModal.jsx` pass it correctly.

---

### 7.6 Pre-existing: `act()` warnings in `useBoard.test.js`

As noted in prior QA reports, four tests in `useBoard.test.js` emit React `act(...)` warnings. Not introduced by this task.

---

## 8. Test Coverage Assessment

### `BlockEditor.test.jsx` — 29 tests in 4 groups

| Group | Tests | Coverage |
|-------|-------|----------|
| Basic rendering | 6 | Null/undefined/empty/falsy content, view-mode buttons, "No description" text |
| Initialization / parseContent | 3 | JSON, plain-text fallback, null → undefined initialContent |
| Edit/read-only mode toggle | 12 | Enter/exit edit mode, `editable` prop, Escape key, `onEditingChange` callback, cancel reset |
| Save / JSON serialisation | 8 | `onSave` called with `JSON.stringify`, loading state, error display, error clear on success |

Coverage is thorough for the implemented behaviour. No tests exercise the `parseContent` fallback in a real BlockNote environment (it is mocked), so the concern in §7.3 is not caught by the test suite.

### `CardModal.test.jsx` — 60 tests (5 new)

The 5 new `CardModal — description` tests verify:
- `BlockEditor` is rendered in place of a static paragraph
- `content` prop passes `card.description`
- `onSave` triggers `onUpdate(card.id, { description: json })`
- Escape does not close modal while `BlockEditor` reports `isEditing=true`
- Escape closes modal after description editing ends

All 5 tests pass. The `BlockEditor` is correctly mocked to isolate `CardModal` behaviour.

---

## 9. Overall Assessment

**Task 14 implementation: CONDITIONAL PASS**

The BlockNote editor is functionally integrated, all tests pass (362/362), lint is clean, and the build succeeds. The core requirements — wrapper component, edit/read-only toggle, JSON save to database, empty state handling, and styling — are all correctly implemented.

**Two issues warrant attention before this can be considered fully compliant:**

1. **No explicit block schema configuration (§7.1 — Medium):** The task explicitly requires configuring the editor with specific block types (headings, paragraphs, bullet lists, numbered lists, code blocks). The implementation relies on BlockNote's default schema instead, which includes all required types but also many others. This is a specification compliance gap, not a functional bug.

2. **Content prop changes not reflected in editor after mount (§7.2 — Medium):** Because `initialContent` is a one-time option, real-time description updates from other clients (delivered via WebSocket) will not update the editor's displayed content unless the component is unmounted and remounted. This is a functional limitation in the context of the app's real-time update architecture.

**Outstanding issues not attributable to task 14:**

| Issue | Severity | Origin |
|-------|----------|--------|
| `act(...)` warnings in `useBoard.test.js` | Low (tests still pass) | Prior task |
| Server tests fail (`express`/`better-sqlite3` not installed) | High (server test suite broken) | Environment / prior tasks |
